texto: She must not give herself up as hopeless, as many a plain girl does, scrape her hair back into an unsymmetrical bundle, have her clothes "cut out with a hatchet and put on with a pitchfork," ill-use her skin with coarse soaps and neglect her figure. Only a beauty may dare all this, and even the loveliest cannot afford it.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Mrs. C.E. Humphry sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “She must not give herself up as hopeless, as many a plain girl does, scrape her hair back into an…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a amor, advice, beauty, útil em momentos de beauty e hair.
